posts.raw_content:
South Carolina RINOs Once Again Betrayed the Base — Get Rid of the RINOS!

On May 12, 2026, five Republican state senators in South Carolina voted with Democrats to block mid-decade redistricting. The 29-17 vote fell short of the two-thirds majority needed to fix unconstitutional, racially gerrymandered maps that protect Democrat Jim Clyburn’s seat. Senate Majority Leader Shane Massey and his allies — including Sean Bennett, Chip Campsen, Tom Davis, and Greg Hembree — killed the chance for a 7-0 Republican congressional delegation in a state that has decisively rejected liberal policies.
It’s just one more example of their deliberate disdain for the Republican platform and the voters who put them in power. South Carolina handed Republicans supermajorities to deliver results: secure borders, election integrity, limited government, traditional values, and to fix the damn roads. Instead, these RINOs obstruct closed primaries that would protect the party from Democrat crossover sabotage. They cling to rigged districts. And they flirt with expanding legalized gambling — casinos and sports betting that prey on working families — while softening on drugs in ways that echo the failures destroying blue cities.
The logic is simple and infuriating. Recent Supreme Court signals have opened the door to correcting race-based maps that no longer reflect South Carolina’s strong Republican tilt, especially in the Upstate and Lowcountry. Fixing them would amplify conservative voices in Washington during critical 2026 midterms. These five chose self-preservation and moderation over victory. Their actions weaken Republican strength in the House when America needs maximum firepower against radical Democrats. Families across the Palmetto State pay the price through diluted representation and policies that erode personal responsibility.
This RINO cancer isn’t confined to Columbia — it infects Washington and threatens the entire country. At the federal level, RINOs in Congress mirror the same betrayal: slow-walking America First priorities, protecting the swamp, and undermining President Trump’s agenda. They balk at bold spending cuts, resist true border security measures, and water down efforts to drain the bureaucratic deep state. Their “bipartisanship” often means compromising conservative principles while Democrats never budge. The Republican leadership won't even let Trump ressess appoint all the nominees they are holding up. It is designed to stall momentum on key issues, erode voter trust, and weaken the Republican Party heading into midterms.
Just like in South Carolina, federal RINOs show open contempt for the platform that delivers wins. They prioritize insider perks and DC cocktail circuits over the working families who demand results. This disdain doesn’t just hurt South Carolina — it drags the nation backward. It hands Democrats victories without them earning a single vote. It suppresses conservative turnout when enthusiasm is most needed. And it signals to the country that even with Republican majorities, nothing fundamental changes.
South Carolinians and Americans nationwide are done with the excuses. These RINOs wear the label but reject the fight. Their obstruction on redistricting, primaries, gambling, and broader conservative reforms proves they fear a strong, unified Republican Party more than they fear Democrat policies.
The RINO era must end now, before they drag the entire Palmetto State and the Country backward. We’re watching, and we’re done waiting. Primary every single one. Replace them with fighters who close primaries, fix the maps, reject vice industries, and deliver unapologetic conservative victories at every level of government. South Carolina is red. America is rejecting the left. It’s time our representatives act like it. No more excuses. No more betrayal!
